fact: In the Northern Hemisphere, this date marks when many trees have lost about half their leaves, creating the perfect crunchy carpet for jumping in leaf piles.
October 21 falls smack in the middle of peak migration season - millions of monarch butterflies are fluttering through Mexico right now on their epic 3,000-mile journey!
This date sits right in the heart of Libra season, which means the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal length three weeks ago.
In northern countries, October 21 is when many animals start growing their thick winter coats, making squirrels look like fluffy gray cotton balls with tails.
October 21 is exactly 294 days into the year - that's almost exactly 42 weeks, which means you've lived through 42 weekend cycles if you started counting on January 1st!
In many places, this date marks when morning frost first appears on car windshields and grass, creating millions of tiny ice crystals that look like nature's glitter.
The Earth is tilting away from the sun so fast around October 21 that northern places lose about 3 minutes of daylight every single day - that's like losing a whole hour every 20 days!
October 21 is when many spiders have grown to their biggest size of the year before winter, which is why you might notice more giant garden spiders in their webs right now.
In apple-growing regions, October 21 hits right during peak harvest time when the fruit has the perfect balance of sugar and crispness - making it nature's candy season!
